archetype: Automated Aquaculture Water Quality Monitoring and Optimization System for Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS)
description: This system monitors and controls water quality in RAS facilities, ensuring optimal conditions for fish and shellfish growth. It integrates with sensors, pumps, and valves to adjust parameters such as pH, temperature, and oxygen levels. The system must account for complex water chemistry interactions and adhere to industry standards for aquatic animal welfare and environmental sustainability.
rules: MUST comply with Global Aquaculture Alliance (GAA) Best Aquaculture Practices (BAP) standards for water quality management
MUST integrate with industry-standard sensors and equipment, such as pH probes and oxygen meters
MUST account for water chemistry interactions, including ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate levels
MUST adjust water treatment parameters in real-time based on sensor data and algorithmic models
MUST maintain a data repository for water quality trends and facility performance metrics
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on manual sampling and testing, as this can lead to delayed detection of water quality issues
DO NOT neglect to account for the specific water chemistry requirements of different aquatic species
DO NOT ignore the impact of water treatment processes on the facility's energy consumption and environmental footprint
key_technical_challenges: Developing a robust and accurate water quality monitoring system that can handle variable sensor data and equipment failures
Creating a sophisticated control algorithm that can adjust water treatment parameters in real-time based on complex water chemistry interactions
Designing a data repository that can store and analyze large amounts of water quality data for trend analysis and facility performance metrics

recommended_stack.language: Python + extensive libraries for scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Flask + lightweight and flexible for real-time data processing
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + robust support for data modeling and querying
recommended_stack.cache: Redis + fast and efficient for caching sensor data and water quality metrics
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ + reliable and scalable for integrating with sensors and equipment

compliance_flags: Global Aquaculture Alliance (GAA) Best Aquaculture Practices (BAP) standards
National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) guidelines for aquatic animal welfare
